title: populus tremula subsp. tremuloides
intro: Populus tremula subsp. tremuloides, also known as trembling or quaking aspen, is a subspecies of the white poplar tree (Populus tremula) native to North America. This deciduous tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Populus tremula subsp. tremuloides include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as support for digestive health and skin issues. Herbal preparations of the bark can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as arthritis and wounds, and promote overall well-being.
